preamble: Voiced dental stops can shift to voiced palatal stops through palatalization, typically triggered by following front vowels or semi-vowels.

examples.note: Historical palatalization of dental /d/ to palatal /ɟ/.
Slender 'd' in Irish is often realized as a palatal stop [ɟ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
